title: The Macro Minute: Technical recession or actual recession?

Howdy team 42, it's your skipper here,
Darius Dale, to present our macro minute
for Thursday, April 3rd, 2025. As
always, we'll start with the executive
summary from today's lead-off morning
note. If you would like to review the
analysis supporting these conclusions
and to see if you need to make any
changes in your portfolio to remain on
the right side of market risk, you need
to be a client of 42 macro. So, today's
key macro question is technical
recession or actual recession?
Uh obviously, we're uh
we're day after liberation day. We'll
find out what that means economically.
So, let's just get into our answers
here. Uh in the Rose Garden event
yesterday that he dubbed liberation day,
President Trump announced a 10% minimum
tariff on all exports to the US plus
reciprocal tariffs on dozens of
countries raising the effective tariff
rate on US imports to over 20% and over
60% on Chinese goods and fulfilling a
core campaign promise.
Bloomberg Economics pegs the new
effective tariff rate at on 3 trillion
of US imports at around 23%, which is
the highest in over a century.
The now infamous 2018 Fed trade model
estimates the 20 percentage point tariff
hike lowers GDP by 2.9 percentage points
and lifts inflation by 1.9 percentage
points.
The same model predicts the unemployment
rate will rise 100 basis points to 5.1%
by mid-2026,
likely signaling a recession if
accurate.
Recent forward guidance from White House
National Economic Council Director Kevin
Hassett was supportive of our transition
from paradigm A to paradigm B view. He
said, "The president believes we can
replace income tax revenue with tariff
revenue with with and if if we can place
income tax revenue with tariff revenue,
we can make everybody better off." And
obviously, everybody being American
citizens. To that point, Senate
Republicans will soon vote on a new
budget resolution that dials back House
favorite spending cuts, extends 2017
Trump's 2017 tax cuts, and lifts the
debt limit by $5 trillion.
The Senate's reconciliation math uses a
fuzzy current policy baseline to nullify
the cost of extending Trump's 2017 tax
cuts, which expire at the end of 2025.
The Senate plan allows 4 trillion in tax
cut extensions and 1.5 trillion in
additional tax relief versus relative to
the House's $4.5 trillion plan.
President Trump backed the Senate
proposal saying it has his complete and
total support.
When the Fed starts implementing the QE
we've been forecasting since last fall,
that may be the final chance you get as
an investor to sell your entire Treasury
bond portfolio at a decent price. FYI,
42 macro clients already did so last
October and we permanently pivoted to
30% in our 60-30-10 KISS portfolio
construction process out of bonds and
into gold. Ag is unchanged and gold is
up 13% since then, outperforming every
asset in the world except Bitcoin. It
will soon overtake Bitcoin, too, if our
kitchen sinking thesis continues to
unfold perfectly according to plan. So,
uh obviously, there's a lot in there uh
even before we transition to our 42
macro dashboard here uh where we wrap up
with a question and or comment from our
community, uh
I just want to say, if you're losing
money today at all,
you don't have to be.
Uh I don't say that with any degree of
arrogance. I say that with all humility
and the you know, the the the the the
plea for investors to start taking their
financial future more seriously. We
built 42 macro to add sophisticated
institutional grade risk management
overlays to investor portfolios, not
just for the institutional investors,
you know, I've been serving across
global Wall Street for the past 16, 17
years, but also the now thousands of
retail investor clients that benefit
from those exact same overlays.
And uh just right you know, wrapping up
with this comment, uh this uh client is
saying this retail investor client is
saying, "I exited all the stock
positions in my retirement account
around a month ago as per Darius's
recommendations. It was tough to do, but
I just sold the risk off risk off call.
Thank you, Darius." So, recall that we
sold all of our stocks uh in KISS uh and
and and um in our KISS portfolio
construction process, which we use to
help our retail investor clients uh stay
on the right side of market risk. We
sold all of our stocks at the beginning
of March on the first trading day uh of
March. Um we still had a tiny bit of a
little bit of Bitcoin left from the
sales that we made back in February in
Bitcoin, uh but by and large, our
portfolio has been cash and gold uh you
know, since the beginning of March. Uh
and our clients are obviously really um
you know, really benefiting from that
especially on a big down day like today
where the rest of the world is waking up
to the kitchen sinking thesis that we
authored last fall. Um you know,
I don't really know how to say this
without sounding arrogant, but by Jove,
we've been on a ridiculously impressive
run. Go back to 3 years ago in 2022
where we pivoted our clients from being
bullish to ragingly bearish in December
of 2021 ahead of the massive meltdown in
markets in 2022.
In January 2023, we pivoted our clients
from being bearish to bullish ahead of a
uh you know, kind of a mini meltdown in
markets in in 2023. I think I want to
say the market was stock market was up
20%, Bitcoin was probably up 50 to 100%.
We were max long heading into 2024 and
generally max long for most of 2024
throughout the raging bull markets in
stocks and Bitcoin in 2024.
And then heading into uh the a late
February or at the beginning of March of
this year, we pivoted our clients from
being bullish to being some version of
max bearish. Um
That's about as good as a run as you're
going to see anybody
in institutional finance have over the
past 3 to 4 years.
Um
really grateful for the opportunity that
we've had to grow our firm. Really
blessed. Thank you, Lord, for giving us
this platform to help people. You know,
I said this to our client the other day,
like I haven't taken a uh pay increase
since we started the firm. Every dollar
we make goes right back into the
business. And that's exactly why we
continue to innovate. That's why you see
this wonderful, amazing dashboard um in
front of our clients that our client
community chat, you know, lives and
breathes in.
That's why we're going to rehaul
overhaul our signal section. That's
coming uh this Monday. I'm excited uh to
roll that out either early next week uh
to our clients and you know, we're
really grateful for the opportunity that
we've had to, you know, really im-
positively impact the lives of thousands
of investors. I mean, this is the ethos
why we started the firm. A life is not
important except in the impact that it
has on other lives. The reason I left my
cushy, high-paying Wall Street job to
start 42 macro, well, there's a bunch of
reasons, but one of the main reasons was
I got sick of making rich people richer.
I wanted to make regular people richer
because I'm from the wrong side of the
train tracks and I want to make sure I
bring those people with me on my way to
the top. And guess what? If I stay alive
long enough, Lord willing,
I'm going to the top. So, uh you can
either hop on the rocket or you can
fight the exhaust. Have a great day.
Cheers.
